date_notes: CBC investigation published Nov 14 2025, ~3 weeks after Back to School Act (EVT-036).
title_en: Investigation finds government's 3,000-teacher strike commitment was pre-existing budget allocation
title_fr: Une enquête révèle que l'engagement de 3 000 enseignants était déjà prévu au budget
description_en: A CBC News investigation reports that the government's commitment to fund 3,000 new teaching positions — presented during the teachers' strike as a new concession and enshrined in the Back to School Act — had already been allocated in the 2025-26 provincial budget passed in March 2025, before the strike began. The budget allocated $750 million for this purpose.

opposition_response.name: Amanda Chapman
opposition_response.description: Chapman states the government was 'misleading' as most Albertans would not have known the hiring was already budgeted.

relations.note: Post-settlement scrutiny of the strike resolution.

verification_notes: Add 2025-26 Alberta budget (Tier 1) as a future R-064 to confirm $750M allocation date directly.

update_log.change: Created from R-060. DID side of SD-016.

Migrated opposition_response from legacy {actor,response_en,source_ref} to canonical {name,display_role,party,description,date,source_refs} (v3.4 §5.1). date set null (not recoverable from legacy record); party derived from actors[] where present.

Normalized claim_contested_by from drift {counter_claim,counter_claim_type,source_ref} to canonical {actor,position,source_ref} (v3.4 §5.1); counter_claim_type preserved in extensions.claim_contested_by_meta. actor extracted from leading clause where unambiguous, else null for human review.

claim_contested_by.position: Education ministry states the 3,000 positions, while budget-funded, represent real new hiring commitments.
